what_it_does: This test script searches the local .data/store/agent_sessions for a real agent-session transcript containing an assistant message with a tool_use content block, captures the session id and tool_use id, constructs a session://<sid>/step/<tool_use_id> address, gates it via cc_gate, resolves the gated address through resolve_address, asserts the resolver returns the real step/session and the same address, asserts a bogus step id raises ValueError, and reports pass/fail.
observations: Real-data driven: it finds an actual agent session with a tool_use step rather than using a mock fixture.
Skips honestly with sys.exit(0) if no qualifying session is found, explicitly avoiding green-paint.
Uses FsStore, resolve_address, _iter_jsonl, and cc_gate from the project runtime/store packages.
Constructs STEP_ADDR and a gate in /tmp/g_bar1, then deletes that directory at the end.
Maintains global PASS/FAIL lists and an ok() helper that prints inline ok/FAIL lines.
Checks that the resolved record contains the same step id, session id, and address as the gated input.
Fail-loud test expects resolve_address to raise ValueError containing 'unknown step' for a bogus tool_use id.
Only scans the first 80 agent-session directories returned by sorted glob.
standouts: BAR 1 contract test: a gated opaque step_address resolves through the unified H1.1 resolver and round-trips.
Honest skip semantics when no real tool_use data is available.
Fail-loud design: unknown step addresses must raise rather than silently fail or guess nearest.
conventions: Module-level docstring includes run command and purpose.
Global PASS/FAIL accumulators with a small ok() assertion helper.
Cleanup with shutil.rmtree of the temporary gates directory.
Uses REPO := ROOT inside an os.path.join call.
concerns: Hard-coded /tmp/g_bar1 path and shutil.rmtree could collide with or erase unrelated data if reused or run concurrently.
Only iterates sorted(glob(...))[:80], so eligible sessions beyond the first 80 are ignored.
Strips .json from the session id but not other possible extensions.
The fail-loud test creates a gate for the bogus address in the same /tmp directory before the final cleanup.
Broad except Exception on store.load_agent_session swallows load errors silently.
No integration with a standard test runner (pytest/unittest); it is a standalone script.
notes: A real-data contract verification that the cc_gate stores a session step address opaquely yet it still resolves and round-trips through the shared H1.1 resolver.
questions: What exact contract does the H1.1 resolver guarantee beyond returning step/session/address fields?
Could agent-session directory names have extensions other than .json that would break the sid derivation?
Is /tmp/g_bar1 safe under concurrent test runs?
Does resolve_address always raise ValueError for unknown steps, or can it raise other exception types?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual behavior: it finds real data, gates a real tool_use step address, resolves it, checks the address round-trips, and verifies fail-loud behavior for unknown steps.
apparent_themes: Unified address grammar and round-trip integrity
intention_signals: Demonstrate that the gate stores step addresses opaquely while remaining resolvable through the single canonical resolver, proving the gate participates in the unified address grammar instead of inventing side identifiers.
novelty: Uses live store data as a fixture rather than mocks, and treats an honest skip as a first-class test outcome.
contribution: A real-data acceptance test that verifies cc_gate stores and resolves session step addresses through the shared resolver, including a round-trip address invariant and a fail-loud unknown-step case.
summary_for_embedding: A real-data test that gates a live session://<sid>/step/<tool_use_id> address, resolves it through the H1.1 resolver, verifies the same address round-trips, and checks unknown step addresses raise ValueError.
intention_for_embedding: Verify BAR 1: the gate's opaque step_address resolves through the unified resolver and round-trips, with honest skip when no real tool_use fixture exists.

symbols.does: Test assertion helper that records pass/fail and prints an inline ok/FAIL line.
symbols.description: The ok function appends the test name to the global PASS list if cond is truthy or to FAIL otherwise, then prints a formatted 'ok' or 'FAIL' line. It optionally includes detail in the output only when the assertion fails. It is the single local assertion primitive used by the BAR 1 verification script.
